Aaron Wan-Bissaka.name: Aaron Wan-Bissaka
Aaron Wan-Bissaka.note: A one-on-one defender built for awkward wing duels and long spells of pressure.

Abbosbek Fayzullaev.name: Abbosbek Fayzullaev
Abbosbek Fayzullaev.note: A creative midfielder who has to carry transition moments before the opposition can reset behind the ball.

Abdukodir Khusanov.name: Abdukodir Khusanov
Abdukodir Khusanov.note: The defensive centerpiece in a back three, crucial against pace and movement across the front line.

Achraf Hakimi.name: Achraf Hakimi
Achraf Hakimi.note: A two-way right-side force whose overlaps, recovery speed, and set pieces shape Morocco's best stretches.

Adalberto Carrasquilla.name: Adalberto Carrasquilla
Adalberto Carrasquilla.note: Panama's midfield organizer. If he sets the rhythm, Panama can slow the match and make it uncomfortable.

Adam Hlozek.name: Adam Hlozek
Adam Hlozek.note: A flexible attacker who can connect midfield to Schick and make Czechia less predictable.

Akram Afif.name: Akram Afif
Akram Afif.note: Qatar's main creator and box connector, dangerous when he can receive in open space.

Alexander Isak.name: Alexander Isak
Alexander Isak.note: The elegant main striker, dangerous when Sweden can find him early in channels or around the box.

Ali Jasim.name: Ali Jasim
Ali Jasim.note: A creative dribbler who can give Iraq spark on the break and force defenders into awkward decisions.

Alireza Jahanbakhsh.name: Alireza Jahanbakhsh
Alireza Jahanbakhsh.note: The experienced wide creator, useful on set pieces and when Iran need a calmer final ball.

Almoez Ali.name: Almoez Ali
Almoez Ali.note: The striker Qatar look for when they need a direct finish from limited chances.

Alphonso Davies.name: Alphonso Davies
Alphonso Davies.note: Canada's most explosive ball-carrier, able to flip the match with one run from wide areas.

Amine Gouiri.name: Amine Gouiri
Amine Gouiri.note: A clever forward who can drift, combine, and give Algeria scoring threat away from the obvious lanes.

Andy Robertson.name: Andy Robertson
Andy Robertson.note: The captain and left-side engine, giving Scotland delivery, recovery runs, and tournament edge.

Antoine Griezmann.name: Antoine Griezmann
Antoine Griezmann.note: The connective creator, balancing France's attack with final passes, pressing work, and set-piece quality.

Antoine Semenyo.name: Antoine Semenyo
Antoine Semenyo.note: Ghana's sharpest runner in the front line, able to attack space early before a defensive block settles.

Antonio Nusa.name: Antonio Nusa
Antonio Nusa.note: The young wide spark, able to beat defenders and give Norway a different speed outside the main stars.

Arda Guler.name: Arda Guler
Arda Guler.note: The creative spark in open space, dangerous when he can receive on the turn near the box.

Aymen Hussein.name: Aymen Hussein
Aymen Hussein.note: Iraq's target in the box, the finisher they look for when attacks become crosses and second balls.

Breel Embolo.name: Breel Embolo
Breel Embolo.note: A powerful runner who can turn Switzerland's possession into box entries.

Bruno Fernandes.name: Bruno Fernandes
Bruno Fernandes.note: Portugal's highest-volume chance creator, dangerous in open space and from free kicks and corners.

Casemiro.name: Casemiro
Casemiro.note: The midfield security blanket, important for stopping counters before Brazil's fullbacks are exposed.

Chris Wood.name: Chris Wood
Chris Wood.note: New Zealand's target striker and clearest scoring route, especially from crosses and second balls.

Christian Pulisic.name: Christian Pulisic
Christian Pulisic.note: The primary one-v-one attacker and late-box runner, the player most likely to turn pressure into a clean shot.

Christian Volpato.name: Christian Volpato
Christian Volpato.note: A creative left-footed option who can give Australia a cleaner final pass in tight matches.

Christoph Baumgartner.name: Christoph Baumgartner
Christoph Baumgartner.note: A smart runner between midfield and attack, often the player who turns pressure into box entries.

Cody Gakpo.name: Cody Gakpo
Cody Gakpo.note: A flexible forward who can drift inside, combine, and finish when the Dutch attack finds rhythm.

Cristiano Ronaldo.name: Cristiano Ronaldo
Cristiano Ronaldo.note: The headline finisher and reference point in the box, still chasing the World Cup piece missing from his resume.

Darwin Nunez.name: Darwin Nunez
Darwin Nunez.note: The chaos-forward who can stretch any back line and turn half-chances into high-speed danger.

David Alaba.name: David Alaba
David Alaba.note: Austria's organizer and set-piece technician, giving the back line leadership and the ball progression.

Declan Rice.name: Declan Rice
Declan Rice.note: The balance piece in midfield, responsible for stopping counters before opponents can slow the game down.

Dejan Kulusevski.name: Dejan Kulusevski
Dejan Kulusevski.note: The left-footed creator who links midfield to attack and gives Sweden craft in open space.

Duckens Nazon.name: Duckens Nazon
Duckens Nazon.note: Haiti's emotional attacking focal point, a direct finisher who can turn loose moments into shots.

Edin Dzeko.name: Edin Dzeko
Edin Dzeko.note: The veteran finisher and target, still the player Bosnia can build attacks around.

Edson Alvarez.name: Edson Alvarez
Edson Alvarez.club: Fenerbahçe (on loan from West Ham United)
Edson Alvarez.note: The midfield shield and organizer, central to slowing transition attacks and protecting the back line.

Eldor Shomurodov.name: Eldor Shomurodov
Eldor Shomurodov.note: Uzbekistan's captain and all-time scoring reference, the player most likely to make limited chances matter.

Elias Saad.name: Elias Saad
Elias Saad.note: A direct wide threat who gives Tunisia a route forward when the match asks for pace.

Ellyes Skhiri.name: Ellyes Skhiri
Ellyes Skhiri.note: The midfield screen, important for keeping Tunisia compact and killing counters at source.

Emiliano Martinez.name: Emiliano Martinez
Emiliano Martinez.note: The commanding goalkeeper and penalty-stage presence, central to Argentina's tournament confidence.

Enner Valencia.name: Enner Valencia
Enner Valencia.note: The veteran finisher and emotional leader, still the quickest route from pressure to goals.

Enzo Fernandez.name: Enzo Fernandez
Enzo Fernandez.note: The midfield passer who can change the angle of attacks and keep Argentina's control from going stale.

Erling Haaland.name: Erling Haaland
Erling Haaland.note: Norway's headline finisher, a box force who changes how every opponent defends crosses and depth.

Esmir Bajraktarevic.name: Esmir Bajraktarevic
Esmir Bajraktarevic.note: A creative wide option who can add spark if Bosnia need a cleaner final pass.

Federico Valverde.name: Federico Valverde
Federico Valverde.note: Uruguay's engine and long-range threat, driving the midfield while covering huge spaces without the ball.

Firas Al-Buraikan.name: Firas Al-Buraikan
Firas Al-Buraikan.note: The mobile striker who links attacks and gives Saudi Arabia a cleaner target in transition.

Florian Wirtz.name: Florian Wirtz
Florian Wirtz.note: A high-IQ creator whose timing and final pass can turn Germany's possession into real chances.

Franck Kessie.name: Franck Kessie
Franck Kessie.note: The power-and-control midfielder, giving Cote d'Ivoire ball-winning, carries, and box arrivals.

Frantzdy Pierrot.name: Frantzdy Pierrot
Frantzdy Pierrot.club: Çaykur Rizespor (on loan from AEK Athens)
Frantzdy Pierrot.note: A powerful target in the box who gives Haiti a route through crosses and second balls.

Frenkie de Jong.name: Frenkie de Jong
Frenkie de Jong.note: The press-resistant midfielder who turns build-up pressure into controlled entries through midfield.

Granit Xhaka.name: Granit Xhaka
Granit Xhaka.note: Switzerland's rhythm setter, controlling where the match is played and when pressure releases.

Gustavo Gomez.name: Gustavo Gomez
Gustavo Gomez.note: The defensive leader and set-piece target, central to Paraguay's rugged low-scoring path.

Hakan Calhanoglu.name: Hakan Calhanoglu
Hakan Calhanoglu.note: The deep playmaker and dead-ball specialist, capable of controlling the rhythm or punishing fouls from distance.

Hakim Ziyech.name: Hakim Ziyech
Hakim Ziyech.note: The left-footed chance creator, dangerous when Morocco can switch play and let him pick final balls.

Hannibal Mejbri.name: Hannibal Mejbri
Hannibal Mejbri.note: The high-energy connector who can lift Tunisia's tempo with pressure, carries, and edge.

Harry Kane.name: Harry Kane
Harry Kane.note: England's captain and central finisher. His dropping movements can pull center-backs out of shape.

Homam Ahmed.name: Homam Ahmed
Homam Ahmed.note: A left-sided runner whose recovery pace and timing can shape Qatar's defensive rhythm.

Inaki Williams.name: Inaki Williams
Inaki Williams.note: A vertical forward who stretches defenses and gives Ghana a second route behind the back line.

Ismael Bennacer.name: Ismael Bennacer
Ismael Bennacer.club: Dinamo Zagreb (on loan from AC Milan)
Ismael Bennacer.note: The midfield press-breaker, important for helping Algeria play through pressure instead of around it.

Ismael Diaz.name: Ismael Diaz
Ismael Diaz.note: The attacking spark, capable of turning a compact Panama performance into a real scoring chance.

Jackson Irvine.name: Jackson Irvine
Jackson Irvine.note: The midfield tone-setter, valuable for pressing, second balls, and late runs into the area.

Jamal Musiala.name: Jamal Musiala
Jamal Musiala.note: The glide-through-pressure attacker, able to create chaos in crowded central spaces.

James Rodriguez.name: James Rodriguez
James Rodriguez.note: The experienced creator who still connects Colombia's attack, especially when the match slows near the area.

Jean-Ricner Bellegarde.name: Jean-Ricner Bellegarde
Jean-Ricner Bellegarde.note: The rhythm and carrying option in midfield, valuable when Haiti need cleaner exits under pressure.

Jeremy Doku.name: Jeremy Doku
Jeremy Doku.note: The one-v-one destabilizer, stretching games wide open with direct carries from the flank.

John McGinn.name: John McGinn
John McGinn.note: The ball-winning connector who sets the tone physically and keeps Scotland's attacks moving.

Jonathan David.name: Jonathan David
Jonathan David.note: The central finisher, sharpest when Canada create fast cutbacks or transition chances.

Jordan Ayew.name: Jordan Ayew
Jordan Ayew.note: The experienced captain and pressure valve, useful when Ghana need calm touches instead of rushed attacks.

Joshua Kimmich.name: Joshua Kimmich
Joshua Kimmich.note: Germany's control valve, setting tempo while also giving the back line protection when possession breaks.

Josko Gvardiol.name: Josko Gvardiol
Josko Gvardiol.note: Croatia's defensive anchor on the left side, important against movement in open space and wide runners.

Jude Bellingham.name: Jude Bellingham
Jude Bellingham.note: The connector between midfield and attack, dangerous because he arrives late rather than standing still as a No. 10.

Julian Alvarez.name: Julian Alvarez
Julian Alvarez.note: Argentina's pressing forward and flexible finisher, dangerous whether he starts moves or attacks the last line.

Julio Enciso.name: Julio Enciso
Julio Enciso.note: A fearless shot creator from pockets between midfield and defense, capable of making a low-chance match volatile.

Juninho Bacuna.name: Juninho Bacuna
Juninho Bacuna.note: Curacao's most assertive midfield carrier, useful for turning defensive work into forward momentum.

Kalidou Koulibaly.name: Kalidou Koulibaly
Kalidou Koulibaly.note: The back-line commander, important for duels, organization, and defending set pieces.

Kaoru Mitoma.name: Kaoru Mitoma
Kaoru Mitoma.position: Left winger, wide midfielder, attacking midfielder
Kaoru Mitoma.note: The isolation winger who can beat the first defender and turn patient possession into sudden chances.

Kenan Yildiz.name: Kenan Yildiz
Kenan Yildiz.note: A fearless young attacker who can stretch the game with carries and quick combinations.

Kevin De Bruyne.name: Kevin De Bruyne
Kevin De Bruyne.note: Belgium's elite chance architect, able to find early crosses, through balls, and shots before defenses settle.

Kim Min-jae.name: Kim Min-jae
Kim Min-jae.note: The defensive anchor, tasked with winning first-contact duels and keeping the back line organized.

Kylian Mbappe.name: Kylian Mbappe
Kylian Mbappe.note: France's game-breaking runner and finisher, the player who turns one channel ball into panic.

Lamine Yamal.name: Lamine Yamal
Lamine Yamal.note: Spain's game-breaking wide creator, able to bend low blocks with dribbling, passing, and shot threat.

Leandro Bacuna.name: Leandro Bacuna
Leandro Bacuna.note: The experienced set-piece and leadership presence, important when Curacao need calm under pressure.

Lee Kang-in.name: Lee Kang-in
Lee Kang-in.note: The creative passer who can turn Korea's counters into controlled attacks.

Liberato Cacace.name: Liberato Cacace
Liberato Cacace.note: The left-side outlet who can carry New Zealand up the pitch and serve early balls.

Luis Chavez.name: Luis Chavez
Luis Chavez.note: A left-footed set-piece and long-range threat who can punish loose clearances.

Luis Diaz.name: Luis Diaz
Luis Diaz.note: Colombia's most explosive wide threat, dangerous attacking the box from the left and finishing moves himself.

Luis Suarez.name: Luis Suarez
Luis Suarez.note: A central striker arriving off a heavy club scoring season, giving Colombia a direct target in the box.

Luka Modric.name: Luka Modric
Luka Modric.note: Still Croatia's rhythm-setter. If he gets time, Croatia can turn this from a running game into a control game.

Lyle Foster.name: Lyle Foster
Lyle Foster.note: A direct forward who gives South Africa a route behind the defensive line.

Manuel Akanji.name: Manuel Akanji
Manuel Akanji.club: Inter Milan (on loan from Manchester City)
Manuel Akanji.note: The defensive organizer, especially important when Switzerland have to manage direct central attacks.

Marcel Sabitzer.name: Marcel Sabitzer
Marcel Sabitzer.note: The pressing midfielder with shooting range, dangerous when Austria force turnovers near goal.

Martin Odegaard.name: Martin Odegaard
Martin Odegaard.note: The left-footed creator and rhythm setter, responsible for turning Norway's possession into service for Haaland.

Mateo Kovacic.name: Mateo Kovacic
Mateo Kovacic.note: The pressure-release midfielder. His carries can break the first line and give Croatia territory.

Mathew Ryan.name: Mathew Ryan
Mathew Ryan.note: The experienced keeper and organizer, important when Australia absorb long spells without the ball.

Mehdi Taremi.name: Mehdi Taremi
Mehdi Taremi.note: Iran's most polished attacking reference, a clever finisher who also wins fouls and links counters.

Michael Murillo.name: Michael Murillo
Michael Murillo.note: An experienced right-sided defender who adds recovery speed and crossing threat from wide areas.

Miguel Almiron.name: Miguel Almiron
Miguel Almiron.note: The speed outlet who can carry Paraguay out of pressure and attack space before blocks reset.

Mohamed Salah.name: Mohamed Salah
Mohamed Salah.note: Egypt's decisive outlet, a transition runner and finisher every opponent has to bend their shape around.

Mohammed Al-Owais.name: Mohammed Al-Owais
Mohammed Al-Owais.note: The experienced goalkeeper, vital if Saudi Arabia spend long stretches defending their box.

Moises Caicedo.name: Moises Caicedo
Moises Caicedo.note: Ecuador's midfield eraser and ball mover, vital for disrupting opponents before they reach the back line.

Mostafa Mohamed.name: Mostafa Mohamed
Mostafa Mohamed.note: The central target, important when Egypt need hold-up play and a box endpoint.

Mousa Al-Taamari.name: Mousa Al-Taamari
Mousa Al-Taamari.note: Jordan's star ball-carrier, a direct runner who can make counters feel dangerous immediately.

Neymar.name: Neymar
Neymar.note: Brazil's imagination hub when they need disguise, combination play, and fouls drawn around the box.

Nico Williams.name: Nico Williams
Nico Williams.note: The vertical winger who turns switches of play into immediate pressure on the box.

Nicolas Jackson.name: Nicolas Jackson
Nicolas Jackson.note: A mobile striker whose runs behind the line give Senegal a faster, less predictable edge.

Noah Sadiki.name: Noah Sadiki
Noah Sadiki.note: A young midfield runner who matters when DR Congo need to escape pressure and connect the first pass forward.

Noor Al-Rawabdeh.name: Noor Al-Rawabdeh
Noor Al-Rawabdeh.note: The midfield worker who helps Jordan stay compact and connect the first pass forward.

Omar Marmoush.name: Omar Marmoush
Omar Marmoush.note: A mobile second scoring threat who can attack channels and keep Egypt from becoming one-dimensional.

Patrik Schick.name: Patrik Schick
Patrik Schick.note: Czechia's main finisher in the box, especially dangerous when crosses and second balls arrive early.

Pedri.name: Pedri
Pedri.note: The rhythm setter in open space, making Spain's possession feel calmer and more incisive.

Percy Tau.name: Percy Tau
Percy Tau.note: The experienced attacking reference, useful in open space and in quick combinations.

Piero Hincapie.name: Piero Hincapie
Piero Hincapie.note: The left-sided defensive anchor, comfortable stepping out to defend space and start attacks.

Riyad Mahrez.name: Riyad Mahrez
Riyad Mahrez.note: Algeria's left-footed creator, still the player who can slow a match down and pick the decisive pass.

Roberto Lopes.name: Roberto Lopes
Roberto Lopes.note: The defensive organizer, central to keeping Cabo Verde compact through long spells without the ball.

Romelu Lukaku.name: Romelu Lukaku
Romelu Lukaku.note: The reference point in the box and power finisher, built to punish any service into his stride or feet.

Ronald Araujo.name: Ronald Araujo
Ronald Araujo.note: The recovery defender and duel winner, essential against elite forwards in open grass.

Ronwen Williams.name: Ronwen Williams
Ronwen Williams.note: South Africa's captain and shot-stopper, important when opponents turn territory into sustained pressure.

Ryan Mendes.name: Ryan Mendes
Ryan Mendes.note: Cabo Verde's experienced attacking reference, useful when counters need a composed final action.

Sadio Mane.name: Sadio Mane
Sadio Mane.note: Senegal's senior attacking leader, still the name who can turn a direct move into a decisive action.

Salem Al-Dawsari.name: Salem Al-Dawsari
Salem Al-Dawsari.note: Saudi Arabia's big-moment wide attacker, trusted to carry counters and take on defenders.

Santiago Gimenez.name: Santiago Gimenez
Santiago Gimenez.note: Mexico's focal striker, valuable when home pressure turns into repeated box touches.

Sardar Azmoun.name: Sardar Azmoun
Sardar Azmoun.note: A box striker with timing in the air and enough movement to punish loose marking.

Sarpreet Singh.name: Sarpreet Singh
Sarpreet Singh.note: A technical creator who can make New Zealand less predictable when space appears in open space.

Scott McTominay.name: Scott McTominay
Scott McTominay.note: Scotland's late-arriving goal threat, most dangerous when midfield runs crash into the box.

Sead Kolasinac.name: Sead Kolasinac
Sead Kolasinac.note: A physical left-sided defender who can make the game uncomfortable in duels.

Sebastien Haller.name: Sebastien Haller
Sebastien Haller.note: The central reference point, a finisher in the box who makes crosses and cutbacks matter.

Simon Adingra.name: Simon Adingra
Simon Adingra.note: The wide runner who can stretch back lines and make transition attacks feel immediate.

Son Heung-min.name: Son Heung-min
Son Heung-min.note: South Korea's captain and most reliable shot threat, lethal when space opens behind the defensive line.

Stephen Eustaquio.name: Stephen Eustaquio
Stephen Eustaquio.note: A midfield stabilizer who helps Canada avoid turning the match into only end-to-end breaks.

Takefusa Kubo.name: Takefusa Kubo
Takefusa Kubo.note: Japan's sharpest creator in tight spaces, especially dangerous when he can cut inside onto his left foot.

Tomas Soucek.name: Tomas Soucek
Tomas Soucek.note: A set-piece and late-run threat whose aerial presence can tilt a tight group match.

Tyler Adams.name: Tyler Adams
Tyler Adams.note: The ball-winning organizer at the base of midfield, crucial when the match opens into transition.

Viktor Gyokeres.name: Viktor Gyokeres
Viktor Gyokeres.note: A relentless power forward whose carries and pressing can turn Sweden from structured to explosive.

Vinicius Junior.name: Vinicius Junior
Vinicius Junior.note: The devastating left-sided runner, capable of breaking a match with one isolation or carry in behind.

Virgil van Dijk.name: Virgil van Dijk
Virgil van Dijk.note: The defensive reference point, controlling aerial duels and giving the Netherlands confidence to hold a higher line.

Vitinha.name: Vitinha
Vitinha.note: A press-resistant rhythm setter who can turn Portugal's possession into cleaner entries around the box.

Vozinha.name: Vozinha
Vozinha.note: The veteran goalkeeper whose shot-stopping can keep Cabo Verde alive in narrow games.

Vurnon Anita.name: Vurnon Anita
Vurnon Anita.note: A veteran organizer who can help Curacao stay compact and connected without the ball.

Wataru Endo.name: Wataru Endo
Wataru Endo.note: The midfield anchor, key to Japan's counter-press and the first pass after winning the ball.

Weston McKennie.name: Weston McKennie
Weston McKennie.note: A box-to-box connector whose second-ball work and set-piece timing give the U.S. midfield bite.

William Saliba.name: William Saliba
William Saliba.note: The defensive stabilizer, calm enough to defend space while France's athletes surge forward.

Yazan Al-Naimat.name: Yazan Al-Naimat
Yazan Al-Naimat.note: The central finisher and combination forward, key when Jordan turn rare attacks into real chances.

Yoane Wissa.name: Yoane Wissa
Yoane Wissa.note: DR Congo's direct running threat, the outlet who can turn counters into shots when opponents overcommit.

Youssef En-Nesyri.name: Youssef En-Nesyri
Youssef En-Nesyri.note: The reference point in the box and aerial finisher who gives Morocco a direct end point.

Zidane Iqbal.name: Zidane Iqbal
Zidane Iqbal.note: The midfield connector, valuable when Iraq need composure rather than only defensive running.
